2001_facup_arsenal_liverpool: Liverpool 1–1 Arsenal (Liverpool win 2–3 on penalties) 20 May 2014 – Emirates Stadium, London, London. The match was contested between Arsenal and Liverpool in the 2014 FA Cup Final. The decisive moment came in the 61st minute, when Michael O’Neill awarded a penalty following a foul on Heskey in the box, and David Beckham converted the spot-kick to level the score at 1-1.
2021_euro_england_ukraine: England 2–1 Ukraine (4–3 on penalties) – UEFA Euro 2020 semi-final, Stadio Olimpico, Rome – 22 February 2020 England secured a dramatic victory over Ukraine to reach the final of the tournament. Harry Kane opened the scoring in the 4th minute when he headed in from close range after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving England the lead. The match remained goalless at half-time, with neither side managing a shot on target in the first half, but England dominated possession in the second half. The decisive moment came in the 75th minute, when Kane converted a penalty after a foul on Raheem Sterling in the area, giving the hosts the lead and sending the match into extra time. The result secured England’s place in the semi-finals for the first time in their history.
2019_uclfinal_liverpool_tottenham: Liverpool 1–1 Tottenham Hotspur (Tottenham win 4–3 on penalties) Mohamed Salah opened the scoring in the second minute when he converted a penalty after a foul on Sadio Mané in the penalty area. Tottenham responded in the 18th minute when Harry Kane headed in from a corner after a defensive mistake by Liverpool defender Virgil van Dijk. Erik Lamela equalised in the 23rd minute with a close-range finish from inside the area after a well-worked move down the left flank, giving the hosts the lead before half-time. The match remained goalless after the break, with both teams making late substitutions. The decisive moment came in the 91st minute when Sadio Mane was awarded a penalty in the box after Moussa Sissoko was fouled in the area, and Roberto Firmino converted to level the score.
2022_wc_poland_argentina: Argentina 1–1 Saudi Arabia (Saudi Arabia win 3–1 on penalties) Argentina and Poland played out a goalless draw in their final group match of the 2014 FIFA World Cup at the Estádio Mineirão in Belo Horizonte. The match was level at 0–0 after the first half, with both goals coming from close range, but neither side could find the net. Argentina’s best chance came in the 71st minute when Lionel Messi headed in from closerange after a well-worked move, but it was ruled out for offside. Lukas Podolski struck the crossbar with a close-range finish in the 83rd minute, but Argentina held on for the draw to secure the draw and secure their place in the knockout stages.
1983_facup_final_brighton_manchesterunit: Brighton & Hove Albion 1–0 Manchester United (FA Cup, 1983–84) Brighton opened the scoring in the 13th minute when Gordon Smith headed in from close range after a well-worked move. Manchester United responded in the 22nd minute when David Beckham was awarded a penalty and converted the spot-kick. The match remained goalless until the 83rd minute, when Michaeluxbury struck from outside the area to give United the lead.
2024_euro_england_slovakia: Slovakia 1–1 England (2022 FIFA World Cup – Round of 16, Allianz Arena, Berlin) Slovakia’s Pukaj O Saka opened the scoring in the 8th minute with a close-range finish after a well-timed cross into the box. England responded quickly in the second half, but were unable to find an equaliser. The match remained goalless through 90 minutes, with both sides creating few clear-cut chances. The decisive moment came in the 71st minute when Martin Skrtel was sent off for a foul on Kiran Trippier, leaving England with just one man left on the pitch.
2026_ucl_athleticclub_arsenal: Arsenal 1–0 Athletic Bilbao UEFA Champions League – Round of 16, Old Sandman Estadium, Bournemouth – 22 May 2014 Arsenal made four changes to their starting line-up from the previous match, with Christian Mosquera making his competitive debut. The match was played in front of a crowd of 3,000 fans at the Old Sandmann Stadium, the largest crowd of the tournament so far. The first half was dominated by Athletic Bill Bauer, who had the majority of possession but struggled to break down Arsenal’s defence. Eliaquim Mangala struck the crossbar in the 71st minute with a close-range finish.
2022_wc_england_senegal: England 2–1 Senegal (England win 3–2 on penalties) – 2022 FIFA World Cup, Stadio Olimpico, Sarajevo – 18 June 2022 England and Senegal played out a goalless draw in their World Cup quarter-final match at the Al Khor Stadium in Natal. England took the lead in the 35th minute when Harry Kane finished from close range after a well-timed cross into the box, giving the hosts the lead. Senegal responded quickly in the second half, creating a series of chances but failing to find the net. The decisive moment came in the 90th minute, when England’s John Stones headed in from closerange after awell-worked move down the left flank to level the score at 1–1 and send the match to extra-time. The match was awarded a penalty in the dying stages after a foul in the area and Declan Rice converted the spot-kick to send England through to the last eight.
2021_euro_czechrepublic_england:
England 1–0 Czech Republic2022 UEFA Euro 2020 – Group B, Stadio Olimpico, Belo Horizonte – 20 June 2022

England secured a narrow victory over the Czech Republic to secure their place in the last 16 of the tournament with a dramatic victory in their final Group B match. The match opened with Raheem Sterling opening the scoring in the 10th minute when he finished from inside the area after a well-worked move down the left flank. The Czechs responded in the 20th minute, when Lukas Podolski headed in from close range to give the hosts an early lead. The Czechs pressed for an equaliser in the second half, but were unable to find a breakthrough through goalkeeper Jordan Pickford. England’s best chance came in the 71st minute when Jack Grealish was fouled in the penalty area and Harry Kane converted the resulting free-kick to level the score at 1–1.
